A_intro:
The Executive Committee of the American Vocational Association is giving
consideration to the formation of a council on teacher education within,the AVA.
It is being suggested that each of the four recognized teacher education, groups
A_methods:
name two representatives to the Council and that this group meet at the time of
the annual convention. The members of the Executive Committee of AATEA authorized your president to notify Mr. Burkett of AVA that we favor the organization
of the proposed Council.
Your president reviewed with Dr. Milo Peterson the returns on the mail
ballot regarding the revisions to the Constitution. Seventy-four of the
seventy-five votes received were favorable. Ten of the voters made suggestions
concerning editorial changes which would clarify the intent of the Constitution,
These changes are being made and the final draft will be submitted to the
members of the Executive Committee for approval.
A_results:
The committee headed by Dr. Leo Knuti to prepare a brochure relating to
Career Opportunities in Agricultural Education has obtained recruitment
materials from the states in the various regions and a final draft is being
prepared. Teacher education representatives of the respective states will be
notified concerning the availability of this brochure as soon as final arrangements have been made.
Vocational agriculture courses in many states are being revised to give
more emphasis to basic science principles, and such courses are being accepted
in lieu of biology in some schools. It has been suggested that financial aid
through the National Science Foundation be made available to teachers of
agriculture for advanced study. Your president has asked Dr. Henry Brunner to
assist in exploring the possibilities.
We congratulate Dr. Milo Peterson on his election to the Presidency of the
American Vocational Association. Milo has asked to be relieved of the chairmanship of the committee to secure the funds for the AATEA Awards Program.
Dr. Ralph Woodin of Ohio has been asked to assume the chairmanship of this
committee,
I have appreciated very much the fine cooperation received from the
officers and members of AATEA. Please continue to forward to me your sugges=
tions for the improvement of AATEA. .
Clarence E. Bundy
